column1: # Live Ball (1920-1945): Increasing competition. Cardinals 6 pennants. 1944: Both NL in WS.
# Integration (1946-1969): Dodgers 6 pennants. Expansion adds Padres/Expos (1969).
# Expansion (1970-1989): 12 teams. Reds 5 pennants. 1981 split season.
# Modern-First (1990-2005): 14-15 teams. Braves 14 straight. 1997/2003 Marlins WS.
# Modern-Resurgence (2006-2016): 15 teams. Cardinals 5 pennants. 2016 Cubs end drought.
# Modern-Dodgers (2017-2025): Dodgers 8 straight NL West. Back-to-back WS. 8 competitive titles.
# Cardinals: Won titles in 6 of last 7 complete decades (1930s
# Giants: 23 pennants from NY era; strong both in NY and SF
# Braves: Atlanta dynasty (1991-2005) most dominant in 1990s
# Cubs: 2016 ended longest WS drought; 17 total pennants
# Reds: 10 pennants; Big Red Machine (1975-76); last title 2005

column2: 2 WS as youngest franchise with fewest games
